title: Would Your Team Use a Dynamic Approval Flow Builder in Monday.com?

posts.author: swapnil_sanu (Swapnil Sanu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com community!

We’re exploring the idea of building a new app for Monday.com: a Dynamic Approval Flow Builder.

Here’s the vision:

Admins create approval flows (like “purchase approvals,” “leave requests,” etc.) for any board.

They select which items and columns should trigger an approval request.

Set custom conditions (e.g., only show the “Request Approval” button for items with “Status = Pending” and “Amount > ₹10,000”).

The button appears automatically when items meet those criteria—no manual setup needed per item.

Approvers get notified, can approve/reject from Monday, and the app auto-tracks the whole process.

Question for you:

Do you face bottlenecks or confusion with manual approval tracking?

Would a simple, dynamic approval flow builder for Monday.com help your team work faster?

What are the approval challenges you have now that this could solve?

If this sounds useful, or if you want to shape its features, please reply below or DM! Your feedback will shape how we build this for real Monday users.

Thanks for reading—looking forward to hearing what you think!

posts.author: zoe.sgt (Zoe | SGTLab)
posts.cookedHtml:
Interesting idea! From what I know, monday’s new Workflow Builder already supports conditional approvals and notifications (like triggering approval requests when status = pending and amount > ₹10,000).

However, it’s currently available only for Pro and Enterprise plans.

Maybe your app could take it a step further, like adding a visual “Request Approval” button or a centralized approval dashboard to track all approvals.
